title: The Last Light of Alder House — Chapter 1: The Letter

preview.text: Maya Chen receives a letter telling her that her grandmother has died and left her a mysterious house on the Oregon coast. She drives to the small town of Alder Bay to sign the paperwork — and discovers that the house has a dark reputation. This curriculum teaches 20 English vocabulary words through an engaging mystery story written for intermediate learners.

description: Learn 20 intermediate English vocabulary words through Chapter 1 of a mystery novel. Across 5 sessions you will study flashcards and read sections of the story, absorbing new words in context as Maya inherits Alder House and travels to the coastal town of Alder Bay.

activities.title: Flashcards: The Letter Arrives
activities.description: Study 4 words: faded, heir, estate, envelope

activities.title: Read: The Letter Arrives
activities.description: Read the opening of the story — Maya receives a surprising letter from a law firm in a town she barely remembers.

data.text:
Maya Chen had not thought about Alder Bay in years. The small town on the Oregon coast was just a faded memory — the smell of salt air, the sound of waves, and the face of a grandmother she barely knew.

So when the letter arrived on a Tuesday morning in March, Maya almost threw it away. It looked official, with a law firm's name printed in dark blue across the envelope: Whitfield & Associates, Alder Bay, Oregon.

She opened it standing at her kitchen counter in Portland, still holding her coffee.

Dear Ms. Chen,

We regret to inform you of the passing of your grandmother, Eleanor Voss, on February 28th. As her sole living heir, you have inherited her property at 14 Cliff Road, Alder Bay, known locally as "Alder House."

We kindly request your presence in Alder Bay at your earliest convenience to discuss the terms of the estate and sign the necessary documents.

Sincerely,
Thomas Whitfield, Attorney at Law

activities.title: Listen: The Letter Arrives
activities.description: Listen to the passage you just read and follow along.

So when the letter arrived on a Tuesday morning in March, Maya almost threw it away. It looked official, with a law firm's name printed in dark blue across the envelope: Whitfield & Associates, Alder Bay, Oregon.

Sincerely,
Thomas Whitfield, Attorney at Law

activities.title: Flashcards: Maya's Decision
activities.description: Study 4 words: curiosity, remarkable, specific, hesitate

activities.title: Read: Maya's Decision
activities.description: Read about Maya's memories, her phone call with the lawyer, and her decision to visit Alder Bay.

data.text:
Maya read the letter twice. She put down her coffee. Her hands were shaking, but she was not sure why.

She had met Eleanor Voss only once, when she was seven years old. Her mother had driven them to Alder Bay for a weekend visit. Maya remembered a tall woman with silver hair and sharp blue eyes. She remembered a large, dark house on a cliff above the ocean. She remembered her mother arguing with Eleanor in the kitchen, their voices low and angry.

They had left the next morning, and her mother never spoke of Eleanor again.

Now her mother was gone too — cancer, three years ago — and Maya was alone. She was twenty-eight, working as a graphic designer for a small company in Portland. She had a cat named Oliver, a one-bedroom apartment, and not much else.

She looked at the letter again. Sole living heir.

A house. On the coast. It was probably falling apart. It was probably worth nothing. But something pulled at her — a feeling she could not name. Curiosity, maybe. Or something deeper.

She called the law firm that afternoon.

"Ms. Chen. Thank you for calling. I'm sorry for your loss."

"Thank you," Maya said. "I didn't really know her, to be honest."

There was a pause. "I see. Well, Eleanor was... a remarkable woman. She lived in Alder House for over fifty years. She was very specific in her will. Everything goes to you — the house, the land, and the contents inside."

"I can come Friday," she said.

After she hung up, Maya sat on her couch and stared at the wall. Oliver jumped into her lap and purred.

"What do you think, Oliver?" she said. "Should we go see what Grandma left us?"

Oliver blinked slowly, which Maya took as a yes.

activities.title: Listen: Maya's Decision
activities.description: Listen to the passage you just read and follow along.

learningSessions.title: Session 3: The Drive to Alder Bay

activities.title: Flashcards: The Drive to Alder Bay
activities.description: Study 4 words: restless, harbor, headland, reveal

activities.title: Read: The Drive to Alder Bay
activities.description: Read about Maya's drive along the coast and her first glimpse of the small, forgotten town.

data.text:
The drive from Portland to Alder Bay took about three hours. Maya left early Friday morning, before the sun was fully up. The highway followed the Columbia River west, then turned south along the coast. The ocean appeared and disappeared between the trees, gray and restless under a cloudy sky.

Alder Bay was not on most maps. It was too small — just a few hundred people living in a town that time seemed to have forgotten. Maya almost missed the turn. A small wooden sign, half-hidden by bushes, read: ALDER BAY — POP. 342.

The road wound down through thick forest and then opened up to reveal the town. It sat in a natural bay, protected from the worst of the ocean storms by two rocky headlands. There was a main street with a grocery store, a post office, a café, and a few other shops. Fishing boats rocked gently in a small harbor. Everything looked old but clean, like a photograph from another time.

activities.title: Listen: The Drive to Alder Bay
activities.description: Listen to the passage you just read and follow along.

There was a main street with a grocery store, a post office, a café, and a few other shops. Fishing boats rocked gently in a small harbor. Everything looked old but clean, like a photograph from another time.

activities.title: Flashcards: Meeting the Lawyer
activities.description: Study 4 words: straightforward, property, maintain, elegant

activities.title: Read: Meeting the Lawyer
activities.description: Read about Maya's meeting with Thomas Whitfield — the paperwork, the keys, and a sealed personal letter from Eleanor.

data.text:
Maya parked in front of the law office, a small white building with a wooden sign. She checked her reflection in the rearview mirror, took a breath, and went inside.

Thomas Whitfield was a man in his sixties with kind eyes and a gray beard. His office smelled like old books and coffee. He shook Maya's hand and offered her a seat.

"Thank you for coming," he said. "I know it's a long drive."

"It's fine. I wanted to see the place."

He nodded and opened a folder on his desk. "The paperwork is straightforward. Eleanor owned the house and two acres of land outright. No debts, no liens. It's all yours, free and clear."

"What's the house like?" Maya asked.

Whitfield leaned back in his chair. "It's old. Built in 1892. Eleanor maintained it well, but it needs work. It's a beautiful property, though. Right on the cliff, overlooking the ocean. There's nothing else like it in town."

He paused, then reached into his desk drawer and pulled out a sealed envelope. It was cream-colored, with Maya's name written on the front in elegant handwriting.

"This is the letter she left for you," he said. "She gave it to me two years ago, with very specific instructions. I was to give it to you only in person, only after her death."

Maya took the envelope. It felt heavy in her hands.

He gave her a set of keys — three old brass keys on a simple ring — and directions to Cliff Road.

activities.title: Listen: Meeting the Lawyer
activities.description: Listen to the passage you just read and follow along.

learningSessions.title: Session 5: The House on the Cliff

activities.title: Flashcards: The House on the Cliff
activities.description: Study 4 words: reputation, cliff, brass, reputation

activities.title: Read: The House on the Cliff
activities.description: Read the final scene — the lawyer's warning, Maya's first sight of Alder House, and the feeling that the house has been waiting for her.

data.text:
"One more thing," Whitfield said as Maya stood to leave. "Eleanor was a private person. She didn't have many friends in town. But the people here respected her. Some of them were... afraid of her, I think."

"Afraid? Why?"

Whitfield smiled, but it didn't quite reach his eyes. "Alder House has a reputation. Old houses always do, especially in small towns. Don't let the stories bother you."

"What stories?"

But Whitfield just shook her hand and opened the door for her. "Welcome to Alder Bay, Ms. Chen. If you need anything, don't hesitate to call."

Maya walked back to her car with the keys in one hand and the envelope in the other. The sky had grown darker, and a cold wind was blowing in from the sea.

She looked up at the cliff north of town. She could see it there — a large, dark shape against the gray sky. Alder House.

It was waiting for her.

And somewhere deep inside, in a place she didn't fully understand, Maya felt that she had been waiting for it too.

activities.title: Listen: The House on the Cliff
activities.description: Listen to the passage you just read and follow along.
